# CommandFramework - LLM reference

> Version: 0.2.0 · Java 25 · Paper 1.21+ and Velocity 3.4 · MIT license
> Full README: https://github.com/HanielCota/CommandFramework/blob/main/README.md
> This file follows the llms.txt convention (https://llmstxt.org).

CommandFramework is an annotation-driven command library for Paper and Velocity Minecraft plugins. You write a Java class with `@Command` and `@Execute`, call `.build()` once, and the framework registers the command through the native Brigadier lifecycle. It validates annotations at compile time, generates descriptor files, and dispatches with per-sender cooldowns, confirmations, rate limits, dependency injection, and middleware.

## Invariants that LLMs must respect

- Always import annotations from `io.github.hanielcota.commandframework.annotation.*`.
- The **annotation processor** is required. Without it, `scanPackage(...)` finds nothing.
- **Do NOT** declare commands in `plugin.yml` or `paper-plugin.yml`. The framework registers them directly.
- **Do NOT** use legacy `JavaPlugin.getCommand(...)` or `CommandMap`. Paper 1.20.6+ Brigadier lifecycle only.
- `@Sender` parameter must be the **first** parameter (or the first unambiguous sender type).
- Only one `@Arg(greedy = true)` per method and it **must** be the last parameter.
- `@Inject` fields must be non-`final`, non-`static`, and the bound type must be registered via `builder.bind(...)` (or be `JavaPlugin` / `ProxyServer`, both pre-bound).
- Cooldowns are only recorded on successful execution - typo-friendly.
- Placeholders use curly braces `{name}` and are substituted BEFORE MiniMessage parsing, so they can safely appear inside `<click>` / `<hover>` tags.

## Installation (Gradle Kotlin DSL, Paper)

```kotlin
repositories {
    mavenCentral()
    maven("https://repo.papermc.io/repository/maven-public/")
    maven("https://jitpack.io")
}
dependencies {
    implementation("io.github.hanielcota.commandframework:paper:0.2.0")
    annotationProcessor("io.github.hanielcota.commandframework:processor:0.2.0")
}
```

For Velocity, swap `paper` for `velocity`.

## Minimal working plugin (Paper)

```java
public final class MyPlugin extends JavaPlugin {
    @Override public void onEnable() {
        PaperCommandFramework.paper(this)
            .scanPackage("com.example.myplugin.commands")
            .build();
    }
}

@Command(name = "heal")
@Permission("myplugin.heal")
public final class HealCommand {
    @Execute public void run(@Sender Player player) {
        player.setHealth(player.getMaxHealth());
    }
}
```

## Minimal working plugin (Velocity)

```java
@Plugin(id = "myplugin", name = "MyPlugin", version = "1.0.0")
public final class MyProxyPlugin {
    @Inject public MyProxyPlugin(ProxyServer server) {
        VelocityCommandFramework.velocity(server, this)
            .scanPackage("com.example.proxy.commands")
            .build();
    }
}
```

## Annotations (exhaustive)

```java
// io.github.hanielcota.commandframework.annotation

@Target(TYPE)    @Retention(RUNTIME)
@interface Command {
    String name();
    String[] aliases() default {};
    String description() default "";
    String permission() default "";
}

@Target(METHOD)  @Retention(RUNTIME)
@interface Execute { String sub() default ""; }  // "" = root; "foo" = /cmd foo; "foo bar" = /cmd foo bar

@Target(PARAMETER) @Retention(RUNTIME)
@interface Sender {}  // Marks the sender parameter. Implicit if the type is unambiguous.

@Target(PARAMETER) @Retention(RUNTIME)
@interface Arg {
    String value() default "";           // Override inferred parameter name.
    boolean greedy() default false;      // If true, consume rest of line as String. Must be last parameter.
    int maxLength() default 256;         // Reject input longer than this. Must be > 0.
}

@Target(PARAMETER) @Retention(RUNTIME)
@interface Optional {
    String UNSET = "\0__commandframework_unset__\0";
    String value() default UNSET;        // Default when arg missing. UNSET = no default (pure java.util.Optional<T>).
}

@Target({TYPE, METHOD}) @Retention(RUNTIME)
@interface Permission { String value(); }

@Target(METHOD) @Retention(RUNTIME)
@interface Cooldown {
    long value();
    TimeUnit unit() default TimeUnit.SECONDS;
    String bypassPermission() default "";
}

@Target(METHOD) @Retention(RUNTIME)
@interface Confirm {
    int expireSeconds();
    String commandName();                 // Label the player types/clicks to confirm.
}

@Target({TYPE, METHOD}) @Retention(RUNTIME)
@interface RequirePlayer {}               // Console is rejected with MessageKey.PLAYER_ONLY.

@Target(METHOD) @Retention(RUNTIME)
@interface Async {}                       // Executor runs on a virtual thread.

@Target(FIELD)   @Retention(RUNTIME)
@interface Inject {}                      // Field-level DI. Resolved from builder.bind(...).

@Target(METHOD) @Retention(RUNTIME)
@interface Description { String value(); } // Shown in auto-generated help.
```

## Builder API - `CommandFrameworkBuilder<S, B>`

Returned by `PaperCommandFramework.paper(plugin)` or `VelocityCommandFramework.velocity(server, plugin)`. All methods return `this`.

| Method | Notes |
|---|---|
| `scanPackage(String pkg)` | Scan a Java package for `@Command` classes. Uses generated descriptors - requires annotation processor. |
| `command(Object instance)` | Register a single pre-built command. Use when DI happens outside the framework. |
| `commands(Object... instances)` | Register several pre-built commands. |
| `bind(Class<T> type, T instance)` | Register a service for `@Inject` and resolver constructors. |
| `resolver(ArgumentResolver<?> r)` | Register a custom argument type. |
| `middleware(CommandMiddleware m)` | Register a middleware (wraps every dispatch). |
| `message(MessageKey k, String template)` | Override one message template. |
| `messages(MessageProvider p)` | Replace the whole provider. Prefer `MessageProvider.fromStringMap(...)` for config-backed. |
| `rateLimit(int commands, Duration window)` | Fixed-window limiter. Default 30 per 10s. Console exempt. |
| `debug(boolean on)` | Log each dispatch phase. |
| `build()` | Validate, register, return `CommandFramework<S>`. |

## Results - sealed `CommandResult`

```java
sealed interface CommandResult permits Success, Handled, Failure, InvalidArgs,
        NoPermission, PlayerOnly, CooldownActive, PendingConfirmation,
        HelpShown, RateLimited {}
```

You rarely inspect them directly - messages and flow are automatic. Tests and middlewares do pattern-match them.

## MessageKey - exhaustive list

Enum `io.github.hanielcota.commandframework.MessageKey`:

| Key | Placeholders | Fires when |
|---|---|---|
| `PLAYER_ONLY` | - | Non-player ran a `@RequirePlayer` command. |
| `NO_PERMISSION` | - | Sender missed `@Permission`. |
| `INVALID_ARGUMENT` | `{name}`, `{input}` | Resolver rejected input. |
| `MISSING_ARGUMENT` | `{name}` | Required arg not supplied. |
| `TOO_MANY_ARGUMENTS` | `{input}` | Extra tokens beyond signature. |
| `COOLDOWN_ACTIVE` | `{remaining}` | Cooldown still active. |
| `COMMAND_ERROR` | - | Executor threw unhandled exception. |
| `CONFIRM_PROMPT` | `{command}`, `{seconds}` | Confirmation requested. Default is clickable. |
| `CONFIRM_NOTHING_PENDING` | - | Confirmation command run with nothing pending. |
| `HELP_HEADER` | `{command}` | Top of auto help. |
| `HELP_ENTRY` | `{usage}`, `{description}` | Per-executor line in help. |
| `UNKNOWN_SUBCOMMAND` | `{typed}`, `{command}`, `{suggestion}` | Did-you-mean. |

## MessageProvider factories

```java
// io.github.hanielcota.commandframework.MessageProvider
static MessageProvider defaults();
static MessageProvider fromMap(Map<MessageKey, String> templates);       // Type-safe.
static MessageProvider fromStringMap(Map<String, String> templates);     // Config-friendly: "no-permission", "NO_PERMISSION", and "no_permission" all map to NO_PERMISSION. Unknown keys ignored. Missing keys fall back to defaults.
```

## Built-in argument resolvers

Available in every framework instance:
- `String` (trimmed, maxLength enforced)
- `boolean` / `Boolean` (accepts true/false/yes/no/on/off)
- `int`, `long`, `double`, `float` (parse + range)
- `java.util.UUID`
- Any `Enum<E>` - suggestions include every constant, case-insensitive
- `java.util.Optional<T>` - wraps any of the above for optional parameters

Paper adds: `org.bukkit.entity.Player`, `org.bukkit.World`.
Velocity adds: `com.velocitypowered.api.proxy.Player`.

## Custom ArgumentResolver

```java
public final class KitResolver implements ArgumentResolver<Kit> {
    private final KitService kits;
    public KitResolver(KitService kits) { this.kits = kits; }
    @Override public Class<Kit> type() { return Kit.class; }
    @Override public Kit resolve(ArgumentResolutionContext ctx, String input) throws ArgumentResolveException {
        return kits.find(input).orElseThrow(() ->
            new ArgumentResolveException("kit", input, "Unknown kit"));
    }
    @Override public List<String> suggest(CommandActor actor, String currentInput) {
        return kits.names().stream().filter(n -> n.startsWith(currentInput)).toList();
    }
}
```

## Middleware

```java
public interface CommandMiddleware {
    CommandResult handle(CommandContext ctx, Chain chain);
    interface Chain { CommandResult proceed(CommandContext ctx); }
}
```

Order of registration = order of execution (first registered runs outermost).

`CommandContext` is a record with these accessors:

| accessor | type |
| --- | --- |
| `actor()` | `CommandActor` |
| `label()` | `String` |
| `rawArguments()` | `String` (single raw tail, NOT `String[]`) |
| `arguments()` | `List<String>` (tokenized, immutable) |
| `commandPath()` | `String` (e.g. `"eco pay"`) |

## CommandActor API

```java
public interface CommandActor {
    String name();
    UUID uniqueId();
    boolean isPlayer();
    boolean hasPermission(String permission);
    void sendMessage(net.kyori.adventure.text.Component message);
    boolean isAvailable();            // false once the player disconnects
    Object platformSender();           // Raw platform object (CommandSender / CommandSource)
}
```

## Testing - `core-testkit`

```kotlin
testImplementation("io.github.hanielcota.commandframework:core-testkit:0.2.0")
```

```java
CommandTestKit env = CommandTestKit.create();
TestFrameworkBuilder framework = env.framework(new HealCommand());
TestPlayer alice = framework.player("Alice").grant("myplugin.heal");
CommandResult result = alice.dispatch("heal", "");
DispatchAssert.assertThat(result).succeeded();
```

Assertions available on `DispatchAssert`: `succeeded()`, `handled()`, `failed(MessageKey)`, `invalidArgs(name, input)`, `noPermission(permission)`, `playerOnly()`, `cooldown(remaining)`, `pendingConfirmation(commandName)`, `helpShown()`.

## Common tasks - recipes

### Subcommands with shared permission

```java
@Command(name = "admin")
@Permission("myplugin.admin")
public final class AdminCommand {
    @Execute public void panel(@Sender Player p) {}
    @Execute(sub = "ban") @Permission("myplugin.admin.ban")
    public void ban(@Sender Player p, Player target) {}
}
```

### Inject a service into a command

```java
PaperCommandFramework.paper(this).bind(EconomyService.class, economy).build();

@Command(name = "bal")
public final class BalanceCommand {
    @Inject private EconomyService economy;
    @Execute public void run(@Sender Player p) { p.sendMessage(Component.text(economy.getBalance(p.getUniqueId()))); }
}
```

### Optional with default

```java
@Execute(sub = "give")
public void give(@Sender Player p, Player target, double amount,
                 @Optional("false") boolean silent) {}
```

### Load messages from YAML (Paper)

```java
this.saveResource("messages.yml", false);
var yaml = YamlConfiguration.loadConfiguration(new File(getDataFolder(), "messages.yml"));
var map = yaml.getKeys(false).stream()
        .collect(Collectors.toMap(k -> k, yaml::getString));
builder.messages(MessageProvider.fromStringMap(map));
```

### Confirm a destructive action

```java
@Execute(sub = "wipe") @Permission("myplugin.admin")
@Confirm(expireSeconds = 10, commandName = "confirmar")
public void wipe(@Sender Player p) { /* ... */ }
// Player sees [Confirm] button -> clicking runs /confirmar within 10s.
```

### Async DB/HTTP inside a handler

```java
@Execute @Async
public void home(@Sender CommandActor actor) {
    UUID playerId = actor.uniqueId();
    Location h = db.loadHome(playerId); // blocking is fine, virtual thread

    Bukkit.getScheduler().runTask(plugin, () -> {
        Player player = Bukkit.getPlayer(playerId);
        if (player != null) {
            player.teleport(h);
        }
    });
}
```

## Frequent LLM mistakes to avoid

1. **Inventing annotations** - the full list is above. `@Route`, `@Handler`, `@Subcommand`, `@Path` do not exist.
2. **Inventing sub-packages** - there is no `io.github.hanielcota.commandframework.message`, `.middleware`, `.resolver`, or `.actor`. Everything is flat: `CommandActor`, `CommandContext`, `CommandResult`, `CommandMiddleware`, `ArgumentResolver`, `MessageKey`, `MessageProvider` all live directly at `io.github.hanielcota.commandframework.*`. Only the **annotations** are under `.annotation.*` and the **platform bridges** under `.paper.*` / `.velocity.*` / `.testkit.*`.
3. **Wrong Maven coordinates** - the published `groupId` is `io.github.hanielcota.commandframework` (NOT `com.github.HanielCota.CommandFramework`, which only matches JitPack's URL path and 404s as a coordinate). Every dependency is `io.github.hanielcota.commandframework:<module>:<version>`.
4. **`ctx.rawArguments()` as `String[]`** - it is a single `String` (the raw, untokenized argument tail). Use `ctx.arguments()` for the tokenized `List<String>`.
5. **Multi-token `@Execute(sub = "player ban")`** - a sub name is a single token. Use a single-token form such as `@Execute(sub = "playerban")` / `player_ban`, or split into separate commands.
6. **`@Async` + `@Sender Player`** - async executors must take `@Sender CommandActor` (not `Player` / `CommandSender` / `CommandSource`). Re-resolve the platform sender inside via `Bukkit.getPlayer(actor.uniqueId())` etc.
7. **Using Bukkit command classes** - do not extend `Command`, `TabExecutor`, `CommandExecutor`. Do not touch `SimpleCommandMap`.
8. **Registering in `plugin.yml`** - skip the `commands:` section. Only `name`, `main`, `version`, `api-version` are required. On Paper 1.20.5+ prefer `paper-plugin.yml` (the modern format used by `examples/paper-sample`); legacy `plugin.yml` still works but triggers a deprecation notice.
9. **Forgetting the annotation processor** - without it, `scanPackage` finds nothing, and `@Arg(greedy = true)` won't be validated until runtime.
10. **`@Sender` on the wrong parameter** - must be first, must be a platform-known type (`CommandActor`, `Player`, `CommandSender` / `CommandSource`).
11. **Treating messages as plain strings** - they are MiniMessage. Use `<red>`, `<click:run_command:'/foo'>`, `<hover:show_text:'bar'>`.
12. **Manual async** - use `@Async` for blocking work, then hop back to the platform main thread yourself before calling thread-confined APIs.
13. **Cooldown before parsing** - cooldown is post-parse. Don't try to apply it manually in the handler.
14. **Final/static `@Inject` fields** - must be plain instance fields.
15. **`ArgumentResolver.type()` returning generic type** - `List<Kit>.class` isn't a thing. Use `Kit.class`.

## Source of truth

- Javadoc: https://hanielcota.github.io/CommandFramework/
- Source: https://github.com/HanielCota/CommandFramework
- Examples: https://github.com/HanielCota/CommandFramework/tree/main/examples
- Changelog: https://github.com/HanielCota/CommandFramework/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
